title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: Class 8 — Profit, Loss and Interest
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Raju buys a bicycle for ₹1,500 and sells it for ₹1,800. What is his profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Profit = SP - CP = 1800 - 1500 = ₹300. Profit% = (300/1500) × 100 = 20%. This is exactly how a shop calculates its margin!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A shopkeeper marks a shirt at ₹800 and offers a 15% discount. What is the selling price?
questions.explanation: Discount = 15% of 800 = ₹120. SP = 800 - 120 = ₹680. Every 'Sale' tag you see in a mall uses this exact calculation!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Priya deposits ₹5,000 in a post office savings account at 7.5% simple interest per year. How much interest will she earn in 3 years?
questions.explanation: SI = (P × R × T) / 100 = (5000 × 7.5 × 3) / 100 = ₹1,125. Post Office savings schemes are among the safest investments for families in India!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A pizza shop buys flour, cheese, and other ingredients worth ₹200 to make 10 pizzas. If they sell each pizza for ₹60, what is the overall profit percentage?
questions.explanation: Total Revenue = 10 × 60 = ₹600. CP = ₹200. Profit = ₹400. Profit% = (400/200) × 100 = 200%. Note: actual restaurants also pay rent, salaries etc. This is why pizza shops don't keep all 200%!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Ananya has ₹10,000. She invests ₹6,000 in FD at 8% simple interest for 2 years, and puts the rest in a piggy bank. How much total money will she have after 2 years?
questions.explanation: FD Interest = (6000 × 8 × 2)/100 = ₹960. FD amount = ₹6,960. Piggy bank = ₹4,000. Total = ₹10,960. This is why financial advisors say: never keep all money in a piggy bank!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: A mobile phone costs ₹15,000. GST of 18% is added. What is the final price you pay?
questions.explanation: GST = 18% of 15000 = ₹2,700. Final price = 15000 + 2700 = ₹17,700. Every electronic purchase includes GST — this is the government's share of every sale in India.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Vivaan earns ₹12,000/month from part-time work. If he saves 25% and spends the rest, how much does he spend annually? What is the direct proportion formula used?
questions.explanation: Monthly spending = 75% of ₹12,000 = ₹9,000. Annual spending = ₹9,000 × 12 = ₹1,08,000. This is direct proportion: spending ∝ income.
questions.subject: Mathematics
